id: bigtech:ill buy 10 of thosenasa science chief yearns for massproduce:2026-05-19T20:43:31.000Z
title: "I'll buy 10 of those"—NASA science chief yearns for mass-produced satellites
author: Stephen Clark
summary_long: There are more opportunities to access space than ever, thanks to a bevy of commercial rockets, some with reusable boosters, led by SpaceX's workhorse Falcon 9. So why is NASA launching fewer telescopes and planetary science missions than it did a quarter-century ago? The answer is complex. It is not necessarily the money. Source: arstechnica.com. Date: 2026-05-19. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: bigtech:two aibased science assistants succeed with drugretargeting :2026-05-19T18:55:56.000Z
title: Two AI-based science assistants succeed with drug-retargeting tasks
author: John Timmer
summary_long: On Tuesday, Nature released two papers describing AI systems intended to help scientists develop and test hypotheses. One, Google's Co-Scientist, is designed as what they term "scientist in the loop," meaning researchers are regularly applying their judgments to direct the system. The second, from a nonprofit called FutureHouse, goes a step beyond and has trained a system that can evaluate biological data coming from some specific classes of experiments. While Google says its system will also work for physics, both groups exclusively present biological data, and largely straightforward hypotheses—this drug will work for that. Source: arstechnica.com. Date: 2026-05-19. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: bigtech:gemini 35 flash might be fast enough for gen ai to make sens:2026-05-19T18:11:50.000Z
title: Gemini 3.5 Flash might be fast enough for gen AI to make sense
author: Ryan Whitwam
summary_long: At last year's I/O event, Google was still talking about the 2.5 branch of Gemini, and what a difference a year makes. We've gone through the 3.0 and 3.1 families since then, and now it's on to version 3.5. Gemini 3.5 Flash is rolling out across a wide range of Google products starting today, and Google again claims this model is even better than its last-gen Pro model. That has been a trend with Google's tick-tock model updates over the past year, but the team says this release is special. Source: arstechnica.com. Date: 2026-05-19. id: space:spacex launches 24 starlink satellites on falcon 9 rocket la:2026-05-20T00:28:12.000Z
title: SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket launch from Vandenberg SFB
author: Will Robinson-Smith
summary_long: Update May 19, 11:40 p.m. EDT (0340 UTC): SpaceX landed its first stage booster on the drone ship. SpaceX launched its Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base Tuesday night to send a batch of its Starlink satellites into low Earth orbit. The mission, dubbed Starlink 17-42, will add another 24 broadband internet satellites to a constellation of spacecraft that consists of more than 10,000 spacecraft. Source: spaceflightnow.com. id: nextfrontiers:hackers found a way around intel cetplatypus locks down libr:2026-05-19T13:40:01.000Z
title: Hackers found a way around Intel CET—PLaTypus locks down library jumps
summary_long: In June 2020, Intel announced the first hardware availability of Control-Flow Enforcement Technology (CET). This hardware-based protection mechanism has been gradually introduced since Intel's 10th and 11th Core generations and is integrated into newer Windows and Linux operating systems. CET is designed to make so-called code-reuse attacks more difficult, in which attackers exploit existing program code to compromise systems. Source: techxplore.com. Date: 2026-05-19. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: nextfrontiers:opensource framework lets drones dodge obstacles in millisec:2026-05-19T19:20:04.000Z
title: Open-source framework lets drones dodge obstacles in milliseconds while minimizing travel time
summary_long: In the aftermath of a devastating earthquake, unpiloted aerial vehicles (UAVs) could fly through a collapsed building to map the scene, giving rescuers information they need to quickly reach survivors. But this remains an extremely challenging problem for an autonomous robot, which would need to swiftly adjust its trajectory to avoid sudden obstacles while staying on course. Source: techxplore.com. Date: 2026-05-19. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.
